text:
SUMMARY

This is AI generated summarization, which may have errors. For context, always refer to the full article.

TOO COOL. South Korean pistol shooter Kim Ye-ji, whose video of breaking a world record at the Olympics with almost no visible reaction, went viral on social media, demonstrates a shooting position at the Incheon International Airport in Incheon, South Korea on August 7, 2024.

Kim Soo-hyeon/REUTERS

MANILA, Philippines – South Korean shooterKim Ye-jilanded her first acting break after her swagger and charisma made her a star in the Paris Olympics.

Kim, who won silver in the women’s 10 meter air pistol event, reportedly will play an assassin inCrush, a spin-off of global film projectAsia, alongside Indian actress Anushka Sen.

“So happy to collaborate with Kim Ye-ji, Olympic silver medalist, the most popular Korean shooting player in the world,” Sen wrote on Instagram.

A post shared by Anushka Sen (@anushkasen0408)

Kim turned into aninternet sensationovernight as her nonchalant style, which featured her wearing black-tinted glasses and her non-shooting hand tucked into her pocket, propelled her to stardom.

Her Olympic stint gained Kim millions of new fans, including Tesla Motors chief Elon Musk.

“She should be cast in an action movie. No acting required!” Musk wrote on X, replying to a video showing Kim competing in the ISSF Baku World Cup in Azerbaijan in May where she won gold.

Plenty of opportunities have come her way after the Olympics as Kim also became an endorser for fashion brands MLB Korea and Givenchy.

A post shared by Singles 싱글즈 (@singlesmagazine)

– Rappler.com

title: South Korean shooter lands acting role after viral Olympic moment
author: delfin.dioquino editor
description: South Korean shooter Kim Ye-ji, who turned into an unlikely star in the Paris Olympics, will reportedly play an assassin in her first acting job
